a: A base class for common types of data that the data detection system matches.
An object that represents a calendar date or date range that the data detection system matches.
An object that contains an email address that the data detection system matches.
An object that contains a flight number that the data detection system matches.
An object that contains a web link that the data detection system matches.
An object that contains an amount of money that the data detection system matches.
An object that contains a phone number that the data detection system matches.
An object that contains a postal address that the data detection system matches.
An object that contains parcel tracking information that the data detection system matches.
Access and utilize common types of data that the data detection system matches.
An extension to the string protocol that scans strings for semantic entities, such as email addresses, phone numbers, URLs, and flight information.
A representation of a match that includes common properties and an enumeration that represents the match type and its specific semantic components.
Values that suggest how to style a highlighted item.
A highlight style that indicates the match is visually hidden from the display.
A highlight style that indicates the match is accentuated in a nonintrusive manner.
A highlight style that indicates the match displays like a regular web link.
An enumeration of types of matches returned by the scanner.
The values the framework returns that represent date components of a calendar event.
A match that the data detector determines represents an email address.
The values the framework returns that represent an airline code and a flight number.
The value the framework returns for a match of a link that contains a valid URL corresponding to the original link.
A match that the data detector determines represents a measurement.
A match that the data detector determines represents an amount of money.
A match that the data detector determines represents a payment identifier.
Values that describe payment system types.
A value the describes the Unified Payments Interface (UPI).
A match that the data detector determines represents a phone number.
A match that the data detector determines represents a postal address.
A match that the data detector determines represents a shipment tracking number.

A match that the data detector determines represents a shipping tracking number.
A set of types of matches that the system can find in a string.
A set of options you can use to refine the behavior of text scanning, and better interpret the semantic domain of the matches.
The name of an airline.
A value that represents the International Air Transport Association (IATA) code of an airline.
A set of types that includes all publicly useable types.
A Boolean value that indicates whether the event is an all-day event.
A number that represents an amount of money.
A value that represents the amount of money in the specified currency.
The type that represents calendar events, such as dates and times, or date and time ranges.
The name of a parcel carrier.
A string that represents the carrier or service name.
The city name in a postal address.
An optional string that may contain the city component of the address.
The country or region name in a postal address.
A string that contains an ISO currency code, which the data detection system identifies from the matched string and user preferences.
A value that represents the currency the data detectors were able to match, though it may be a best guess.
A property that contains the type and semantic data found in a match.
The creation date of the scanned string.
An optional value that represents the language code of a specific locale.
A locale region that’s relevant to the scanned string.
A date that represents the date of the scanned string.
The type that represents email addresses.
A string that represents an email address.
The value the framework returns for a match on an email address.
A date that represents the end of the event.
An optional date that may contain the end date of the event.
The time zone for the event’s end date.
An optional time zone object that may contain the time zone of the end date of the event.
The type that represents flight numbers.
A string that represents a flight number.
A value that represent the flight number, excluding the airline code.
A string that contains the full string of the address.
An identifier suitable to initiate a payment transaction.
Initializes a new data detector.
A Boolean value that indicates whether the event is an all-day event.
A string that categorizes a phone number, such as Home or Work.
A string that categorizes an email address, such as Home or Work.
An optional string that may contain a value suitable to populate a label when creating a contact.
A value the framework returns that may contain a value suitable to populate a label when creating a contact.
An optional string that may contain a value suitable to populate a label when creating a contact.
The type that represents links, not limited to web links.
A substring that the data detection system identifies from an original string as a common type of data.
The type that represents measurements, such as distances and weights.
A function that returns a measurement object initialized with the specified dimensions.
The type that represents monetary amounts.
The type that represents payment identifiers, such as Universal Payments Interface (UPI) identifiers.
A string that represents a phone number.
The type that represents phone numbers.
A string that contains the phone number the data detector found.
An array of dimensions someone can consider for use in a measurement.
The type that represents postal addresses.
The postal code in a postal address.
An optional string that may contain the postal code component of the address.
A value that suggests a highlight style for a match.
The range of characters in the original text corresponding to the match.
An optional string that may contain the region component of the address.
An optional string that may contain the standard ISO 3166 region code of the address.
The type that represents shipment tracking numbers.
A date that represents the start of the event.
An optional date that may contain the start date of the event.
The time zone for the event’s start date.
An optional time zone object that may contain the time zone of the start date of the event.
The state name in a postal address.
An optional string that may contain the state component of the address.
The street name in a postal address.
An optional string that may contain the street component of the address.

The number the carrier uses to track the package.
A property that may contain a URL that someone can use to track the progress of the delivery.
The type of payment system.
An address for a web resource, such as a webpage or image.
A valid URL that corresponds to the original link.
